reviewer: claude (interactive session, not automated API call)
gate2_note: No OA candidates pursued for this paper's flags — the two TOPIC_MISMATCH findings (refs 4 and 13) were unambiguous from the abstract alone and did not need full-text confirmation to reach high confidence.
pipeline_bugs_found_during_this_run: Book-chapter citation ('Walsh PC... In: Walsh PC, Retik AB... Campbell's Urology 7th Edition') resolved to a DIFFERENT same-author same-year journal article with a similar title, passing the confidence bar despite being the wrong source. Verified by direct Crossref lookup before fixing. Fixed by rejecting 'In: ... (Eds)' citations outright in ref_resolver.py.

Unit superscript 'kg/m[2]' (kg/m-squared) misread as a citation to reference 2 — same failure class as the earlier author-affiliation-superscript bug, different trigger. Found but NOT fixed this session (logged as an open item in STATUS.md).
notes: Highest-confidence finding across all 3 papers: ref [13] (Steiner 1994) is a cadaver anatomy study with zero patients, cited for a specific clinical RCT claim (n=237 vs 97, 3-month continence rates) that appears nowhere in it. Reference-list parsing verified correct against the raw source text -- this is a miscitation in the original manuscript, not a pipeline error. Also note: this paper's 41% unresolved-reference rate is a genuine data-quality finding (heavy OCR noise in the source: 'cantinence', 'asisted', 'Europan', 'radikal', 'lacalized'), not a pipeline bug -- kept in the calibration set deliberately rather than swapped for an easier paper.
